children.heading: Clean hydrogen; hydrogen
children.label: 1 Clean hydrogen; hydrogen
children.text: The terms “clean hydrogen” and “hydrogen” mean hydrogen produced in compliance with the greenhouse gas emissions standard established under , including production from any fuel source.section 16166(a) of this title

children.heading: Fuel cell
children.label: 2 Fuel cell
children.text: The term “fuel cell” means a device that directly converts the chemical energy of a fuel, which is supplied from an external source, and an oxidant into electricity by electrochemical processes occurring at separate electrodes in the device.

children.label: A
children.text: is rated at more than 8,500 pounds gross vehicle weight;

children.label: B
children.text: has a curb weight of more than 6,000 pounds; or

children.label: C
children.text: has a basic vehicle frontal area in excess of 45 square feet.

children.heading: Infrastructure
children.label: 4 Infrastructure
children.text: The term “infrastructure” means the equipment, systems, or facilities used to produce, distribute, deliver, or store hydrogen (except for onboard storage).

children.heading: Light-duty vehicle
children.label: 5 Light-duty vehicle
children.text: The term “light-duty vehicle” means a motor vehicle that is rated at 8,500 or less pounds gross vehicle weight.

children.heading: Task Force
children.label: 7 Task Force
children.text: The term “Task Force” means the Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technical Task Force established under .section 16155 of this title

children.heading: Technical Advisory Committee
children.label: 8 Technical Advisory Committee
children.text: The term “Technical Advisory Committee” means the independent Technical Advisory Committee established under .section 16156 of this title
